mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 8.0 percent signals accelerating labor market recovery, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives equity valuations higher over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or zero forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year backtest, or the cross-sectional predictive power drops below 0.02 Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and housing starts indicates broad-based economic acceleration. This joint demand surge lifts corporate revenue guidance and equity prices over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns fail to exceed the median market return when both proxies trend upward simultaneously, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization. This supply-side improvement reduces input cost pressures and supports equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if INDPRO increases while S&P 500 forward returns remain negative in more than 60% of observations, or if the rolling 1-year Spearman correlation drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in retail sales data demonstrates resilient consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate cash flows and positive equity returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: The forward return distribution shows no statistically significant difference between high-growth and low-growth retail sales periods (p > 0.05), or the out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above a 2.5 percent annualized threshold, inflation expectations accelerate and force monetary tightening. This compression of risk appetite drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if equity returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL crosses the threshold, or if the predictive Rank IC remains below 0.04 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion. This dual improvement reduces wage-price spiral fears and lifts equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60% of instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes in a multivariate model.

mechanism_narrative: Diverging industrial production and housing starts indicate supply chain bottlenecks colliding with cooling construction demand. This mismatch triggers margin compression and negative equity returns over the subsequent week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises while HOUST falls for two consecutive months
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered episodes, or if the cross-sectional predictive power drops below 0.02 Rank IC over any 2-year window.
